questions.question: Define a parameterized constructor and explain why it is used with an example.
keyPoints: Parameterized constructor accepts arguments for initializing data members
Provides flexibility to create objects with specific values
Example: class Rectangle { int length, breadth; Rectangle(int l, int b): length(l), breadth(b) {} }

questions.question: Explain constructor overloading with a suitable C++ program example.
keyPoints: Constructor overloading is defining multiple constructors with different parameters
Compiler selects appropriate constructor based on arguments
Example showing 3 constructors: default, single-parameter, two-parameter
Program demonstrates different object creation scenarios

questions.question: What is an initialization list in constructors? Illustrate with an example.
keyPoints: Initialization list follows constructor name with colon
Members initialized before constructor body executes
Syntax: Constructor() : member1(val1), member2(val2) {}
More efficient than assignment in body

questions.question: What is the difference between default and parameterized constructors?
keyPoints: Default constructor takes no parameters; parameterized takes arguments
Default provided by compiler if no constructor defined
Parameterized allows custom initialization values
Multiple constructors possible through overloading

questions.question: Explain the copy constructor with its syntax and usage.
keyPoints: Copy constructor initializes object from another object of same class
Syntax: ClassName(const ClassName& obj)
Called when: object passed by value, returned by value, or explicitly copied
Performs member-wise copy

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'explicit' keyword with constructors?
keyPoints: Prevents implicit type conversions using single-parameter constructors
Ensures constructor is called only explicitly
Prevents accidental type conversions
Syntax: explicit ClassName(type param)

questions.question: Explain the order of execution of constructor body and initialization list.
keyPoints: Initialization list executes first
Constructor body executes after member initialization
Members initialized in order of declaration in class
Not in order specified in initialization list

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate parameterized constructors with initialization list. Create a class 'Complex' with real and imaginary parts. Include constructors: default, parameterized, and copy constructor. Show object creation using all constructors.
keyPoints: Class Complex with private members real, imag
Default constructor initializes to 0,0
Parameterized constructor accepts two parameters
Copy constructor accepts const reference
Demonstrate all three in main()
Proper output showing all object states

questions.question: Explain constructor overloading with a C++ program. Create a class 'Employee' with data members: name, id, salary. Provide multiple constructors to initialize objects with different combinations of values.
keyPoints: Define Employee class with appropriate data members
Constructor 1: Default - set default values
Constructor 2: Parameterized with name and id
Constructor 3: Parameterized with all three parameters
Show constructor calls in main()
Explain which constructor is invoked for each case

questions.question: What is a copy constructor? Explain its need, syntax, and when it is invoked. Write a program demonstrating shallow copy vs deep copy issues with default copy constructor.
keyPoints: Copy constructor initializes new object from existing object
Need: creating independent copy with same values
Syntax: ClassName(const ClassName&)
Invoked when: pass by value, return by value, explicit copy
Shallow copy: copies pointer address, not data
Deep copy: copies data to new memory
Program demonstrating both approaches

questions.question: Discuss the importance of initialization list over assignment in constructor body. Also explain the order of member initialization in C++ with an example demonstrating why order matters.
keyPoints: Initialization list directly initializes members before body
Assignment in body: default init + then assignment (two operations)
Required for const and reference members
Required for base class constructors
Members initialized in declaration order, not list order
Example showing consequences of wrong assumption about order
